_comment: Canonical YouTube algorithm signal data. Structured for use by seo-intelligence-engine. NOT keyword data — this is algorithm weight and best-practice data. Updated when source-registry sources (youtube-help-recommendations, youtube-creator-blog, youtube-help-analytics) detect changes. Source IDs are back-references to source-registry.json.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Design thumbnail and title together as a unit. Thumbnail drives the first glance; title confirms the premise. For a new channel in a competitive niche, above 5% CTR on home/browse is strong. Test thumbnails by publishing, waiting 48 hours, and swapping if CTR is below 3%.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Clickbait that does not match the video content. YouTube surveys viewers post-watch; a clickbait mismatch depresses the satisfaction score and reduces future recommendation.
algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Current as of mid-2026. YouTube reweights signals without public notice — verify source monthly.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Maximize total minutes watched per video, not just completion percentage. A 20-minute video watched 50% (10 minutes) earns more signal than a 5-minute video watched 100% (5 minutes). Do not artificially shorten videos to boost completion rate at the cost of watch time.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Padding video length with filler to increase watch time. YouTube's satisfaction signal (post-watch survey) will decline, net-negating the watch time gain.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: The shape of the retention curve matters more than the final percentage. A flat line at 40% is a stronger signal than a line that drops from 80% to 20%. Hook within the first 30 seconds. Add re-engagement moments every 3 to 5 minutes to flatten the slope.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: A sharp retention drop at a specific timestamp is a solvable problem. Identify what is happening at that timestamp in the video and address it in future content.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Publish when the audience is most active. Check YouTube Studio audience tab for the creator's specific audience activity pattern. Encourage early engagement in the first 60 seconds of the video: tell viewers what to do (comment below, save this video) explicitly and specifically.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Generic CTAs ('like and subscribe if you enjoyed this'). Specific CTAs ('comment your biggest thrift store challenge below') drive higher engagement rate.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: High-CTR thumbnails are more likely to be session starters because they are surfaced to users not already in a YouTube session. A new channel can disproportionately benefit from this signal by investing heavily in thumbnail quality.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Underinvesting in thumbnail design when the channel is new. Session starts compound — a video that starts sessions continues to receive home feed distribution weeks after publishing.

algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: End screen content should be contextually relevant to the video that just ended. A viewer who just watched a fall mantel video is most likely to click a related fall decor video or a 'how I thrifted everything on this mantel' follow-up. Match the end screen video to the viewer's demonstrated interest.
algorithm_signals.trap_to_avoid: Defaulting to the most recent upload as the end screen video regardless of topic relevance.

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Whether the Short keeps the viewer in the Shorts feed after it ends. A Short that holds a viewer for two or more subsequent Shorts earns outsized distribution. End the Short cleanly so the viewer does not leave the feed.
shorts_algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Primary signal as of 2025. Replaced swipe-through-rate as the leading signal.

avd_targets.sub_30_seconds: approximately 65% AVD to clear distribution gate
avd_targets.30_to_60_seconds: approximately 50% AVD to clear distribution gate

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Design Shorts to hit the AVD threshold for the length chosen. Sub-30-second Shorts with 65%+ AVD advance to Phase 2 distribution. Under-threshold Shorts are suppressed from broader distribution.
shorts_algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Threshold values confirmed from multiple independent channel analyses. Verify against youtube-creator-blog.

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: A rewatch within 2 seconds of video end is counted as a partial new view. Design Shorts with a loop point: the last frame flows naturally back to the first frame so a replay feels intentional. A reveal-based ending that makes the viewer want to watch again scores highest.

As of 2025, comments carry more weight than raw likes as a Shorts ranking signal. A Short that prompts a reply or question outperforms one that collects passive likes. Prompt specific questions: 'What would you put on a dark fall mantel?' not 'Leave a comment below.'
shorts_algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Signal shift confirmed mid-2025. Verify monthly.

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: What the viewer does after the Short ends is weighted over raw watch time. Subscribe, click to channel page, continue watching Shorts from the same creator — all positive. Leave YouTube — neutral. Verbal CTA to click through to the full video or subscribe can drive positive post-watch behavior.
Content propagation signal. Weighted alongside replays. Shareable Shorts (funny, surprising, educational, or 'show this to someone' moments) compound faster.

For accounts under 50,000 subscribers, Shorts with original voiceovers outperform Shorts using trending audio in recommendation distribution. Trending audio may still support discoverability via the audio page, but original voice wins for algorithmic recommendation.
shorts_algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Signal added March 2026. Check youtube-creator-blog for updates as channel grows past 50K.

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: A consistent series format earns algorithmic preference. A viewer who watches a second Short from the same creator in one session signals strong audience-fit and earns the channel continued distribution in the topic cluster. Name the series and use consistent visual branding.
shorts_algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Signal preference for series confirmed 2025. Current as of mid-2026.

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Still important — first-frame design reduces swipe-away — but behavioral signals from viewers who stay now carry more weight. The critical hook window is 1 to 3 seconds but is no longer the dominant distribution signal.
shorts_algorithm_signals.freshness_note: Demoted from primary to secondary in 2025. Session contribution is now primary.

shorts_algorithm_signals.current_best_practice: Shorts algorithm weights metadata far less than long-form YouTube. Title keywords still affect discoverability in YouTube Search and Google search, but behavioral signals are approximately 80% of the distribution decision. Use the same title keyword discipline as long-form, but do not expect keywords alone to drive distribution.

requirements: 10 million valid Shorts views in prior 90 days
ypp_shorts_path.note: Separate from long-form YPP path (1,000 subscribers + 4,000 watch hours)

shorts_monetization.shorts_watch_hours: Do NOT count toward the 4,000-hour long-form YPP threshold

revenue_share.shorts: 45% to creator from Shorts ad revenue pool
revenue_share.long_form: 55% to creator from long-form ad revenue

phases.name: Cold seeding
phases.description: 50 to 500 viewer test pool; approximately 70% non-subscribers. Short is evaluated against AVD and swipe-through thresholds. Shorts that clear the threshold advance to Phase 2.
phases.gate: AVD threshold (65% for sub-30s, 50% for 30 to 60s)

phases.name: Watch time gate
phases.description: Broader distribution within topic cluster. Comments, saves, and shares are measured here to determine the ceiling for Phase 3.

phases.name: Topic clustering
phases.description: Long-tail distribution to high-intent topic audiences. A top-performing Short can extend distribution for 3 to 6 weeks, unlike feed-based platforms that decay in 24 to 72 hours.
phases.gate: Session contribution — does the viewer continue watching Shorts after this one?

freshness_decay.implication: Unlike long-form evergreen content, Shorts have a decay window. Plan publishing cadence to sustain a constant presence rather than treating each Short as a permanent asset.

shorts_signals.current_best_practice: A Short that gets replayed signals strong entertainment or utility value. Design Shorts with a loop point: the last frame should flow naturally back to the first frame so a replay feels intentional. Completion rate is the dominant signal for Shorts distribution.
A viewer who swipes away immediately signals low relevance. The first frame is the thumbnail for Shorts. Text overlay and strong visual in the first frame reduce immediate swipe-through.

ctr_benchmarks._note: All benchmarks labeled [estimated, unverified]. Verify against the creator's actual YouTube Studio data before making recommendations. These are niche-wide starting points only.
